programs.title: Study Programs
programs.subtitle: Each program gives students deep religious knowledge, research skills, and a culture of service.

items.title: Islamic Studies
items.desc: A comprehensive program for future theologians and imams with deep study of Islamic sciences.

items.title: Quran Ijazah
items.desc: A program for memorizing the Quran, mastering tajweed, and preparing for ijazah.

items.title: Arabic Language
items.desc: An intensive course from beginner level to confident reading of religious texts.

items.title: Imam Preparation
items.desc: A practical program for mosque service, khutbah culture, and community work.

items.title: Theology
items.desc: Academic study of Islamic theology, religious studies, and contemporary religious discourse.

about.title: About the Institute
about.subtitle: The Husamuddin as-Syganaqi Institute brings together traditional Islamic education, academic responsibility, and spiritual formation.
about.history: The institute was created to raise the quality of religious education in Kazakhstan and prepare knowledgeable, responsible specialists who benefit society. The learning process combines classical texts, modern academic methods, and the mentorship tradition.
about.mission: To prepare specialists with deep Islamic knowledge, strong character, and the ability to work wisely with society.
about.vision: To become a respected center of quality religious education in Kazakhstan and develop international academic links.

values.title: Trust
values.desc: To transmit knowledge responsibly and preserve spiritual heritage with care.

values.title: Quality
values.desc: To maintain high standards of curriculum, discipline, and student experience.

values.title: Service
values.desc: To direct acquired knowledge toward public benefit.

admission.title: Admission
admission.subtitle: The admissions office will help you choose a program, prepare documents, and understand the next steps.
admission.form_subtitle: Fill in the fields. The application is saved to Firebase and WhatsApp opens contact with the admissions office.

admission.success_desc: The admissions office will contact you soon.
admission.whatsapp_text: Assalamu alaikum! I would like to learn more about admission to the institute.

steps.desc: Fill out the website form or write on WhatsApp.

steps.desc: An admissions specialist will explain programs and documents.

steps.title: Submit documents
steps.desc: Prepare your documents and send them to the admissions office.

steps.title: Interview
steps.desc: A conversation about preparation level and learning goals.

steps.title: Exam
steps.desc: Taking the entrance exam for the chosen program.

news.title: News
news.subtitle: Important events, announcements, and student life updates.

fallback.title: The institute opened the new academic year
fallback.excerpt: Students were introduced to academic rules, study priorities, and spiritual formation activities.
fallback.content:
The institute held a ceremonial opening of the new academic year. Teachers emphasized responsibility on the path of knowledge and the importance of spiritual formation.

This year the program content has been updated, with expanded Arabic, Quranic sciences, and Islamic studies classes.

fallback.title: Student Quran recitation competition concluded
fallback.excerpt: Participants were evaluated in tajweed, memorization, and qiraat culture.
fallback.content:
Students demonstrated memorization, adherence to tajweed rules, and recitation culture.

Winners received certificates, and top participants were offered individual mentorship.

fallback.title: Arabic language course enrollment is open
fallback.excerpt: The course is for applicants who want to understand religious texts in the original language.
fallback.content:
Enrollment for the Arabic language course is open. The program covers the path from beginner level to text analysis.

Participants will study sarf, nahw, reading, translation, and speaking practice.

gallery.title: Gallery
gallery.subtitle: Institute building, classrooms, library, and student life.

testimonials.name: Alikhan
testimonials.text: The institute teaches not only knowledge, but also responsibility, adab, and service culture.

testimonials.name: Nursultan
testimonials.text: Teachers track each student's pace and pay close attention to memorization and tajweed.

testimonials.name: Medina
testimonials.text: Classes are systematic, clear, and truly help with religious texts.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.subtitle: Short answers about admission, programs, and student life.

items.question: What documents are required for admission?
items.answer: You need an ID copy, education document, photo, medical certificate, and application form.

items.question: Is dormitory accommodation available?
items.answer: Yes. Out-of-city students can receive housing consultation; availability is confirmed during admission.

items.question: Can I start Arabic from zero?
items.answer: Yes. The Arabic course starts from basic level and gradually leads to reading religious texts.

items.question: How do I apply to the Quran Ijazah program?
items.answer: Leave an application, pass a preparation-level interview, and receive an individual study plan.

items.question: What is the class format?
items.answer: Core programs are full-time; some courses may be organized with a flexible schedule.

assistant.fallback: Short answer: I can help with admission, programs, Quran Ijazah, Arabic, and contacts. For details, write to the admissions office on WhatsApp.
assistant.out_of_scope: I answer only questions about Husamuddin as-Syganaqi Islamic Institute: programs, admission, faculty, contacts, events and institute life.
